verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is explicitly documented as a soluble protein lacking a transmembrane domain, distinguishing it from IZUMO1/2/3 which are TM proteins. Evidence points to it being an intra-acrosomal protein in sperm: both isoforms are lost after the acrosome reaction (consistent with luminal content release, not PM-anchored display), and sonication-induced acrosome disruption causes its loss from sperm fractions. No GPI anchor, no TM domain, no documented outer-leaflet PM pool. Pre-no checklist: (1) no cell-state-induced ecto-pool documented for IZUMO4; (2) no tissue-restricted outer-leaflet display — sperm expression is intra-acrosomal, not PM-displayed; (3) not a lysosomal TM protein; (4) no documented PM minority pool or vesicular cycling; (5) no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering — IZUMO4 associates with IZUMO1 complexes in sperm but as a soluble intra-acrosomal component, not stably anchored to the outer leaflet. NCBI nuclear annotation and HPA cytoplasmic-in-seminiferous-duct annotation both align with non-surface biology. No surface-directed therapeutic programs identified.
